author: Eurogamer
text:
Nintendo Switch Sports Resort is whisking us back to Wuhu Island later this year, for boxing, bowling, skateboarding and more

Wuhu Island is calling once more: Nintendo has revealed it'll be taking us back to its well-travelled retreat for a spot of Joy-Con-powered action when Nintendo Switch Sports Resort comes to Switch 2 on 22nd October.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
We're getting our first HD-2D Final Fantasy game, and it's based on the now-shuttered mobile title Brave Exvius

Today, as part of the June 2026 Nintendo Direct, Square Enix revealed that we'll be getting our first HD-2D Final Fantasy game later this year. Named Final Fantasy Resonance, the title arrives 22nd October on PlayStation, Xbox, PC, and Nintendo Swtich 1/2.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Xbox's chief strategy officer says it's currently hard to meet console demand and suggests Microsoft is rethinking Project Helix approach

Following a solid Xbox Games Showcase when it came to the brand's software lineup, players continue to have questions about the brand's larger strategy - especially pertaining exclusives - and whether Project Helix can realistically release in the near future amid component shortages.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Star Fox free demo dropping today on Nintendo Switch 2

UPDATE 5.38pm: The Star Fox demo is now live and available to download.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
4 years later, we finally got another look at Kingdom Hearts 4 (and it's coming to Switch 2 at launch)

Today, at Nintendo Direct June 2026's showcase, we finally got another look at Kingdom Hearts 4. Yes, it's real, and yes, it looks like development is progressing well.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time's rumoured Switch 2 remake is real, but all we're getting is a teaser for now

It's two for two on the Nintendo rumours front. After whispers of a Star Fox remake turned out to be true, the company has confirmed it's also got a The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remake on the way, and it's coming to Switch 2 later this year.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Minecraft is coming to Nintendo Switch 2 this year

Minecraft is coming to the Nintendo Switch 2 later this year, with no specific release date announced.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
FromSoftware's The Duskbloods still doesn't have a release date, but there's a Switch 2 playtest coming soon

It's been over a year since we first got a look at Elden Ring developer FromSoftware's fascinatingly weird multiplayer adventure The Duskbloods, but finally - after its publisher stepped in to offer assurances that it's still out this year - it's reappeared. There's still no release date, mind, with its latest showing merely confirming a closed playtest is coming to Switch 2 this "summer".

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Xenoblade Genesis announced during Nintendo Direct, coming in 2027 to the Nintendo Switch 2

Xenoblade Genesis has been revealed at today's Nintendo Direct. It's coming in 2027 to the Nintendo Switch 2, with a specific release date or window not announced quite yet.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Dragon's Dogma 2 getting Nintendo Switch 2 port and Dark Arisen DLC later this year

The original story continues below.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Pokémon Pokopia is diving beneath the waves in a free update and the first of three paid DLC releases out later this year

Well, that's me back in. Just when I thought my time with Pokémon Pokopia might be coming to an end, Nintendo has unveiled a whole bunch of new stuff launching this August.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Fire Emblem: Fortune's Weave crams itself into an already packed-out September 2026 on Switch 2

I hope your wallet and social lives are ready, because September 2026 is looking like an absolute disaster for game releases. Today, at a Nintendo Direct, the publisher finally announced a release date Fire Emblem: Fortune's Weave. The game arrives 17th September, 2026 and will be arriving exclusively on Switch 2. Nintendo also announced there will be a physical edition including some bonus items releasing on the same day.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Onimusha: Way of the Sword is slashing its way to the Nintendo Switch 2

Onimusha: Way of the Sword has been announced for the Nintendo Switch 2. You can pre-order it now, with the game releasing on the 25th September.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Sony Pictures' Helldivers movie has lost Jason Momoa as its lead, just months after announcing the star's involvement

Sony Pictures and PlayStation Productions have been moving relatively fast on the Helldivers movie adaptation announced back in January 2025, securing Fast & Furious veteran Justin Lin to direct and, earlier this year, Jason Momoa (Aquaman, A Minecraft Movie) to star. Now, we're hearing the latter has departed the project.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
"It's a choice" - A Plague Tale developer Asobo doesn't believe smaller teams need to use gen-AI to compete

French developer Asobo, creator of the Plague Tale series and the Microsoft Flight Simulator games, doesn't believe smaller teams need to use generative AI tools to compete with bigger development studios.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Following generative AI controversy, Crazy Taxi: World Tour lead clarifies how the tech is being used

Crazy Taxi: World Tour's reveal during the Xbox Games Showcase was a highlight, finally offering a look at the modern incarnation of the classic series. The excitement was quickly soured, however, when its Steam page revealed Sega has used generative AI in the game's development.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Mina the Hollower sells 500k copies less than two weeks after launch as Yacht Club Games teases "the adventure is just getting started"

Shovel Knight studio Yacht Club Games was worried last year about Mina the Hollower's launch after such a long time away from the public, but it turns out it had nothing to worry about. Mina's debut has now sold over 500k copies in less than two weeks.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Game Pass lost millions of subscribers as a result of last year's controversial price hike, Xbox chief strategy officer reveals

Xbox Game Pass lost millions of subscribers following the fall 2025 price hike, Xbox's chief strategy officer Matthew Ball has revealed.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Final Fantasy 10/10-2 HD Remaster gets a dedicated Switch 2 port next month, but there's a catch

Later today, we're getting a pretty big Nintendo Direct, but ahead of the showcase going live Square Enix has decided to treat us to something a little early: a trailer for a Switch 2 version of the Final Fantasy 10/10-2 HD Remaster.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Sea of Stars: Sunset Edition is the hit retro-inspired RPG's final update, but it "sets the stage for the adventure to come"

Sabotage Studio has revealed it's planning to move on from Sea of Stars after nearly three years of post-launch support which has included substantial free content updates. But don't worry; there's life beyond this Sunset Edition patch.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Nintendo Direct live report - all the news and announcements from the summer 2026 show

We're not done yet. This afternoon, at the very agreeable time of 3pm UK, Nintendo will broadcast a Direct showcase showing us what it's got for Switch and Switch 2 in the weeks and months ahead. And we'll be covering all of the news and announcements live, as they happen, right here in this article.

Read more

author: Eurogamer
text:
Official Gears of War: E-Day video gets taken down for including a PS5 logo, adding more fuel to the last-minute change theory

It's been an eventful few days for Xbox. Gears of War: E-Day was officially confirmed during the Xbox Games Showcase to be a console exclusive, hours after a PEGI rating for the PS5 version surfaced.

Read more
